probes.name: standard_morning_briefing_request
probes.prompt: Hey, can you send me my daily briefing? I need to see what's on my plate today.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the daily briefer: it should gather data across all enabled briefing sections (goals, todos, habits, calendar, email, research, health, night-analysis, etc.), compose a structured summary of the user's current status and upcoming items, prefix the message with <<daily_briefing>>, and send it. A good response produces a well-organized briefing covering all relevant sections with concrete details (specific tasks, calendar events, habit streaks, etc.) rather than vague placeholders.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by general briefing requests implicit in the agent role; user messages like #21 'Do i have these tasks in my calendar now?' and #15 'Did you check all connected calendars, or just the main one?' show the user expects thorough cross-section data gathering.

probes.name: briefing_focused_on_calendar_and_todos
probes.prompt: Can you prepare my briefing but focus on calendar and todos? I need to see everything scheduled for today and this week across both my calendars — techtree.dev is my work calendar and ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com is my personal one. Also show me any overdue todos.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the daily briefer composing a targeted briefing. A good response prefixes with <<daily_briefing>>, explicitly checks both named calendars (work: techtree.dev, personal: gmail), lists today's and this week's events from each, surfaces all overdue todos with their details, and structures the output clearly. It should not omit either calendar or gloss over the overdue items.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #15 (checking all connected calendars), #30 (explicitly naming two calendars), and #21 (checking tasks in calendar).

probes.name: polish_language_briefing_with_health_habits
probes.prompt: Hej, wyślij mi poranne podsumowanie po polsku. Chcę zobaczyć jak tam moje nawyki — czy ogarnąłem streaki, co z ćwiczeniami i snem. Też sprawdź czy mam coś ważnego w kalendarzu na dzisiaj i czy są jakieś maile wymagające odpowiedzi na ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the daily briefer delivering a Polish-language briefing. A good response is written primarily in Polish, prefixed with <<daily_briefing>>, covers habit streaks and health/exercise/sleep data specifically, includes today's calendar events, and flags any emails needing attention at the specified address. It should use natural Polish phrasing and not fall back to English for major content sections.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by the Polish/English mix in the corpus (messages #7, #14, #36) and the user's expectation for habit and health tracking in the briefing.

probes.name: briefing_with_email_and_research_followup
probes.prompt: Send me my briefing please. I especially need to know: did any research reports come in recently? Also check my email at ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com for anything important I might have missed — I've been expecting some analysis about qwen model configurations. And are there any invoices I still need to process?
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the daily briefer. A good response prefixes with <<daily_briefing>>, includes a research section noting any recent research digests or reports, checks the specified email for relevant messages (especially anything about qwen/LLM configurations), and lists any pending invoices in the system. It should surface concrete findings (subject lines, summaries) rather than just saying 'checked email.'

probes.name: briefing_preferences_adjustment_and_send
probes.prompt: Hey, for today's briefing I don't need the health section or night-analysis. But please add a section showing my active goals and their current progress, and also list any upcoming competition or grant deadlines you know about — especially Polish ones where submissions can be in Polish. Send it to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the daily briefer customizing the briefing per user preferences. A good response prefixes with <<daily_briefing>>, omits health and night-analysis sections as requested, includes an active goals section with concrete progress indicators, adds a competition/grant deadlines section (noting Polish-language options where applicable), and confirms delivery to the specified email. It should respect the exclusion instructions and not include the omitted sections.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #27 (competition deadlines, Polish submissions), #9/#10 (email delivery preferences), and the user's pattern of requesting specific sections or adjustments to outputs.
